_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 5-3 Adult Tachycardia (Revised 7/28/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Cardiac Protocol Section 5-3: Tachycardia — MDHHS Approved 7/28/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY. Aliases: SVT, VT/V-Tach, A-Fib with RVR. Protocol for persistent tachycardia (ventricular rate ≥ 150/min) where tachycardia is believed to be the PRIMARY cause of symptoms. NOT intended for tachycardia secondary to underlying conditions (dehydration, trauma, sepsis, toxins). For rates < 150 believed to be causing symptoms, contact Medical Control. Adenosine is ONLY used for regular monomorphic tachycardic rhythm.

sections.title: General Assessment
points: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY
Follow General Pre-Hospital Care-Treatment Protocol
Identify and treat reversible causes
Determine if patient is stable or unstable

Unstable: hypotension, acutely altered mental status, signs of shock, significant ischemic chest discomfort, shortness of breath, or pulmonary edema likely due to the arrhythmia — unstable patients usually have ventricular rate > 150 BPM
NOTE: Unstable patients with compensatory sinus tachycardia may resemble tachycardic arrhythmias but should NOT be treated as such — treat the underlying cause
Adenosine is ONLY used for regular monomorphic tachycardic rhythm
Consult Medical Control for complex patients where cause of arrhythmia is not obvious

sections.title: UNSTABLE — Cardioversion
points: Prepare for immediate cardioversion — in conscious patients consider sedation prior to cardioversion per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
Perform synchronized cardioversion according to manufacturer recommendations

If unable to deliver synchronized cardioversion in polymorphic VT (including Torsades): defibrillate (cardiovert WITHOUT synchronization) according to manufacturer recommendations or device maximum energy dose
If patient does not convert at maximum energy: contact Medical Control for additional orders

sections.title: STABLE — Narrow Complex: Regular and Narrow (SVT, A-Flutter)
points: If at any point patient becomes unstable: proceed to UNSTABLE section and perform synchronized cardioversion
Start IV NS KVO — large bore antecubital IV preferred
Obtain 12-lead ECG
Contact Medical Control for guidance as needed

Perform Modified Valsalva Maneuver with Postural Modification: (1) place patient in semi-Fowler's position; (2) instruct patient to forcefully blow into a 10 mL syringe for 15 seconds; (3) rapidly lower patient's head to horizontal while SIMULTANEOUSLY elevating legs for 60 seconds — do NOT perform carotid massage; provide continuous cardiac monitoring and run ECG strip during procedure
For suspected SVT not converting with Valsalva: administer adenosine 6 mg rapid IV push through most proximal injection site, followed IMMEDIATELY by 20 mL NS flush
Adenosine may allow flutter waves to be visible indicating A-Flutter — treat as irregular and narrow rhythm below
If conversion does not occur after first adenosine: administer adenosine 12 mg IV using same rapid push technique followed by 20 mL NS flush
If SVT persists after adenosine: treat per MCA selection (see mca_selections_required)

sections.title: STABLE — Narrow Complex: Irregular and Narrow (A-Fib/A-Flutter)
points: For suspected A-Fib/A-Flutter per MCA selection: consider medication administration per MCA selection with Medical Control contact if indicated per MCA selection
Treatment indicated if heart rate is persistently above 125 BPM AND patient is symptomatic from arrhythmia
Consider dehydration, hypovolemia, etc., as causes of compensatory tachycardia before treating
For suspected A-Flutter: treat as irregular and narrow rhythm (same MCA medication box as A-Fib)

sections.title: STABLE — Wide Complex: Regular Wide QRS (V-Tach, SVT/A-Flutter with Aberrancy)
points: For suspected V-Tach: administer amiodarone or lidocaine per MCA selection

If V-Tach persists: contact Medical Control and per Medical Control direction administer amiodarone 150 mg IV over 10 minutes as needed to maximum 450 mg OR lidocaine 0.5–1.0 mg/kg IV push every 5–10 minutes to maximum 3 mg/kg
For suspected SVT with aberrancy: treat as regular and narrow rhythm as above
For suspected A-Flutter with aberrancy: treat as irregular and narrow rhythm as above
NOTE: Wide complex regular tachycardia may represent SVT with aberrancy — contact Medical Control and consider adenosine

sections.title: STABLE — Wide Complex: Irregular Wide QRS (Torsades, A-Fib with Aberrancy)
points: For suspected torsades de pointes: administer magnesium sulfate 2 gm IV over 10 minutes (best accomplished by adding 2 gm to 100 or 250 mL NS)
For suspected atrial fibrillation with aberrancy: follow irregular and narrow complex treatment as above

mca_selections_required.item: Persistent SVT (Regular Narrow) — Medication After Adenosine Failure
mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select medication for SVT persisting after adenosine, AND whether Medical Control contact is required prior to administration

options: Contact Medical Control prior to medication administration
Medication administration without Medical Control contact

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select medication for symptomatic A-Fib/A-Flutter with HR persistently > 125 BPM, AND whether Medical Control contact is required

options: Contact Medical Control prior to medication administration
Medication administration without Medical Control contact

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select antidysrhythmic for suspected V-Tach

key_drugs.name: Adenosine 6 mg IV (SVT — First Dose)
key_drugs.dose: 6 mg rapid IV push through most proximal injection site; followed IMMEDIATELY by 20 mL NS flush
key_drugs.route: IV (most proximal injection site — rapid push)
key_drugs.indication: Regular monomorphic tachycardia (SVT) not converting with Valsalva — ONLY for regular monomorphic rhythms
key_drugs.contraindications: Irregular or polymorphic rhythms; Wolff-Parkinson-White with A-Fib
key_drugs.side_effects: Transient asystole, flushing, chest pressure, dyspnea
key_drugs.notes: Must be given as rapid IV push at most proximal port. If A-Flutter identified by flutter waves becoming visible, treat as irregular narrow rhythm.

key_drugs.name: Adenosine 12 mg IV (SVT — Second Dose)
key_drugs.dose: 12 mg rapid IV push; followed IMMEDIATELY by 20 mL NS flush
key_drugs.route: IV (most proximal injection site — rapid push)
key_drugs.indication: SVT not converting after adenosine 6 mg
key_drugs.side_effects: Transient asystole, flushing, chest pressure, dyspnea
key_drugs.notes: Same rapid push technique as initial dose.

key_drugs.name: Diltiazem 15–20 mg IV (Rate Control)
key_drugs.indication: Persistent SVT after adenosine OR A-Fib/A-Flutter with HR > 125 — per MCA selection
key_drugs.contraindications: Hypotension (SBP < 90); WPW; V-Tach; severe LV dysfunction
key_drugs.notes: MCA must select diltiazem AND whether Medical Control contact required before administration.

key_drugs.name: Verapamil 5 mg IV (Rate Control)
key_drugs.indication: Persistent SVT or A-Fib/A-Flutter — per MCA selection
key_drugs.contraindications: Hypotension; WPW; V-Tach; severe LV dysfunction; beta-blocker use within last 2 hours
key_drugs.notes: MCA must select verapamil AND whether Medical Control contact required.

key_drugs.name: Amiodarone 150 mg IV (Stable Tachycardia)
key_drugs.dose: 150 mg IV over 10 minutes — add to 100 or 250 mL NS; if V-Tach persists per Medical Control: repeat 150 mg IV over 10 min up to maximum 450 mg
key_drugs.indication: Stable V-Tach (per MCA selection) OR A-Fib/A-Flutter rate control (per MCA selection for A-Fib)
key_drugs.contraindications: Severe sinus node dysfunction; 2nd or 3rd degree AV block without pacemaker
key_drugs.notes: Best accomplished by adding 150 mg to 100 or 250 mL NS infused over ~10 minutes.

key_drugs.name: Lidocaine 1 mg/kg IV (V-Tach)
key_drugs.dose: 1 mg/kg IV — if V-Tach persists per Medical Control: 0.5–1.0 mg/kg IV push every 5–10 minutes to maximum 3 mg/kg
key_drugs.side_effects: CNS toxicity at high doses (seizures, altered mental status)
key_drugs.notes: Maximum total dose 3 mg/kg. Repeat doses only per Medical Control direction.

key_drugs.name: Magnesium Sulfate 2 gm IV (Torsades)
key_drugs.dose: 2 gm IV over 10 minutes — add to 100 or 250 mL NS
key_drugs.indication: Suspected torsades de pointes (irregular wide QRS)
key_drugs.side_effects: Flushing, hypotension, respiratory depression at high doses
key_drugs.notes: Best accomplished by adding 2 gm to 100 or 250 mL NS infused over ~10 minutes.

deterioration_flags: Patient becomes unstable (hypotension, AMS, shock, ischemic chest pain) — immediate synchronized cardioversion; consider sedation
Polymorphic VT/Torsades — UNSYNCHRONIZED defibrillation (cannot sync on polymorphic); magnesium sulfate 2 gm for Torsades
SVT with adenosine reveals A-Flutter waves — treat as irregular narrow (A-Fib/A-Flutter path)
Wide complex regular tachycardia — may be SVT with aberrancy; contact Medical Control; consider adenosine
Patient does not convert at maximum energy — contact Medical Control for additional orders
Hypotension after rate control medication — monitor closely; if SBP < 90 treat per Shock protocol
scope_notes: This protocol is PARAMEDIC USE ONLY
Valsalva maneuver with postural modification: Paramedic
Adenosine 6 mg and 12 mg IV: Paramedic
Diltiazem, verapamil, amiodarone, lidocaine: Paramedic — per MCA selection
MCA must select antidysrhythmic for each rhythm type AND whether Medical Control contact required for narrow complex medications
